decomposition_notes: Multi-goal split along the three orthogonal seams from the Sketch section. G1 (events + queue) emits notification_requested into Oban; G2 (preferences UI) lets users opt in to channels and configure DND; G3 (email rendering + dispatch) consumes the queue and emails out. G1 and G2 are independent and can be claimed in any order. G3 depends on G1 — claim G1 first, let its queue contract land in main, then claim G3. G2 is fully parallel.

goals.title: Wire notification event detection and Oban queue
goals.why: Approval lag p50 is 28h because time-sensitive events only surface in-app; events must reach an out-of-band channel via a deduping queue.
goals.what: Emit notification_requested from approval lifecycle / comment mentions / dependency-unblocked, and land them on an Oban queue keyed by recipient + event class so a recipient never gets the same event twice.
goals.description: Independent of UI and email work; produces the queue contract both downstream goals depend on.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
Approval lifecycle emits notification_requested when an approval is owed
Comment mentions emit notification_requested at create time
Dependency-unblocked emits notification_requested
Oban queue dedupes by (recipient_id, event_class)
pitfalls: Do not introduce a new background-job library; reuse the existing Oban setup

tasks.title: Define notification_requested event shape and module
tasks.description: Add MyApp.Notifications.Event struct with recipient_id, event_class, payload, occurred_at. Used as the queue input contract.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Event struct defined
Unit test asserts struct construction + required fields

tasks.title: Add :notifications Oban queue config
tasks.description: Register the new :notifications queue in the existing Oban config with a sensible concurrency limit.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Queue is supervised
Concurrency limit set to a value that respects SMTP relay limits

tasks.title: Emit notification_requested from approval lifecycle
tasks.description: Hook the existing approval-state-transition path so an 'approval owed to X' transition emits a notification_requested event to the queue.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Approval owed transition emits event
Unit test asserts an event is enqueued when the approval is created

tasks.title: Emit notification_requested from comment mentions
tasks.description: On comment create, scan for @mentions and emit one notification_requested event per mentioned user.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Mention scanning emits one event per unique user
Unit test covers single mention, multi-mention, self-mention (suppressed)

tasks.title: Emit notification_requested when a dependency is unblocked
tasks.description: When a task's last blocking dependency completes, emit notification_requested for the task's owner.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Dependency-unblock transition emits event
Unit test covers single-dep and chain-of-deps cases

tasks.title: Implement queue dedupe by (recipient_id, event_class)
tasks.description: Before persisting an enqueued event, look up existing pending events for the same recipient + event class and collapse the payload into the existing job.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Duplicate events within a window collapse
Unit test asserts two enqueues of the same recipient+class produce one job

goals.title: Build user notification preferences UI
goals.why: Users must explicitly opt in to channels (CAN-SPAM + GDPR); without preferences, nothing can be sent.
goals.what: Add a preferences LiveView, the user_notification_preferences schema, per-event-class opt-in toggles, per-user DND window, and email-address override.
goals.description: Independent of G1 and G3. Can be claimed in parallel with either.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
Preferences LiveView lets users opt in per event class
DND window persisted per user
Email-address override defaults to account email
Unsubscribe link in every notification routes back to this UI
pitfalls: Do not send any email to a user who has not explicitly opted in

tasks.title: Add user_notification_preferences schema and migration
tasks.description: Schema fields: user_id, channel (string), event_class (string), enabled (bool), dnd_start, dnd_end, email_override.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Schema + migration land
Default dnd window is 22:00-08:00 in the user's TZ

tasks.title: Add MyApp.Accounts.NotificationPreferences context
tasks.description: Context functions: get_preferences/1, update_preferences/2, channel_enabled?/3, dnd_active?/2. The dispatcher (G3) calls into this to decide whether to send.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
All four functions exist and have tests
channel_enabled? defaults to false for unrecognized channels

tasks.title: Scaffold preferences LiveView mount and basic form
tasks.description: Mount loads the user's preferences via the context; render shows a per-event-class table with opt-in checkboxes.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Route /settings/notifications maps to the LiveView
Form renders one row per event class
Saving updates the database atomically

tasks.title: Add DND time-window controls to preferences form
tasks.description: Add two time-picker inputs (start, end) for the user's DND window; persist on save.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Two time inputs render
Crossing midnight is supported (e.g., 22:00-08:00)
DND window persists across reloads

verification_steps.step_text: Set DND window 22:00-08:00 and confirm dnd_active? returns true at 23:00

tasks.title: Add email-address override input + unsubscribe-link route
tasks.description: Email override input defaults to the account email; unsubscribe link uses a signed token route that opens the preferences page with the relevant channel toggle pre-focused.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Email override saves and is used by the dispatcher in G3
Unsubscribe route validates the signed token before showing the form

goals.title: Render notification emails and dispatch via Oban worker
goals.why: Once events land on the queue (G1) and users have opted in (G2), the dispatcher reads the queue and emits actual emails.
goals.what: Build the email-rendering pipeline (shared shell + per-event-class templates), wire the Oban worker that drains the :notifications queue, and add the per-message unsubscribe footer.
goals.description: Depends on G1 (the queue contract). Claim G1 first; this goal becomes claimable once G1's queue + dedupe land in main.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
Three event-class templates render (approval, mention, dependency-unblock)
Worker drains the queue and dispatches via the existing Mailer
Unsubscribe footer present in every email
Worker respects DND windows and channel opt-in from G2's preferences context
pitfalls: Do not bypass the existing audit-log infrastructure — every notification dispatch is logged
Do not send to a user whose preferences disable the channel

tasks.title: Define the email shell template (header, footer, unsubscribe link)
tasks.description: Shared HTML+text shell that wraps each event-class template. Includes the CAN-SPAM-compliant unsubscribe link with a signed token.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Shell renders for both HTML and plain-text variants
Unsubscribe link includes a signed user+channel token

tasks.title: Render approval-owed email template
tasks.description: Per-event-class template for the approval flow. Plain text first; minimal HTML wrapper around it.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Template renders with the approval payload
Unit test asserts the rendered subject and body match the expected snapshot

tasks.title: Render mention email template
tasks.description: Mention notification template; shows the comment excerpt + a deep link.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Template renders
Unit test covers escaping of user-supplied comment text

tasks.title: Render dependency-unblocked email template
tasks.description: Per-event-class template for the dependency-unblock flow.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Template renders
Unit test asserts the rendered body names the unblocked task

tasks.title: Implement DispatchWorker that drains :notifications and respects preferences
tasks.description: Oban worker that pulls one job at a time, checks MyApp.Accounts.NotificationPreferences for channel_enabled? and dnd_active?, renders the appropriate template, and dispatches via the existing Mailer.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Worker honors channel opt-in (skips if disabled)
Worker honors DND window (delays the job if active)
Audit-log entry written for every dispatch
Unit tests cover the opt-out, DND-active, and happy-path branches
